title: Blechnum gilliesii
intro: Blechnum gilliesii, also known as the Rough Fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Blechnaceae, native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, with reported benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Blechnum gilliesii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Blechnum gilliesii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments.
